page: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is a shared-core virtual machine belonging to the High Performance server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs consisting of 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, backed by a 16 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is limited to 2.0 GB of RAM, yielding 1.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of a 60 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The instance demonstrates strong single-core compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier at 3206.39. However, multi-core CPU performance drops to an average level of 6339.69. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score and cached small-block read bandwidth both fall into the bottom 25%. Large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain average at 41.62 GB/sec and 33.03 GB/sec, respectively.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and low memory density, this server is constrained for memory-intensive or high-throughput applications. This is reflected in its poor database operations score and static web serving throughput, both ranking in the bottom 10%. It is best suited for lightweight, budget-conscious workloads such as basic utility scripting, single-threaded tasks, small-scale development environments, or simple text processing where high memory capacity and dedicated CPU resources are not required.
description: The Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is a shared-core virtual server from the High Performance family, featuring an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 2 vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes a bundled 60 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks show a stark contrast: single-core compute performance is highly competitive, placing in the top 10% under stress-ng testing, whereas multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are average to weak. Database operations and static web serving throughput are particularly limited, scoring in the bottom 10%. Consequently, this budget-friendly instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic utility scripts, and small-scale development environments rather than memory-intensive or high-concurrency production workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is a shared AMD EPYC-Rome server with 2 vCPUs, 2 GB RAM, and 60 GB NVMe storage. It offers strong single-core performance but is constrained by low memory and weak database throughput.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is a shared AMD EPYC server with 2 vCPUs, 2 GB RAM, and 60 GB NVMe SSD, suited for lightweight, budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core AMD EPYC instance featuring fast NVMe storage and strong single-core compute for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC-Rome architecture with 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GB RAM
Bundled 60 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Constrained memory bandwidth and low database operations throughput
Appropriate for lightweight scripting, development, and single-threaded tasks
